title: The NamiMAGI Theory Speculation
wikitext:
[]: The "NamiMAGI" Theory Speculation             Quote Post by eth1023 » Thu Apr 03, 2014 12:06 pm There have been multiple people posting topics talking that each of the "nami" pilots (Rei, Asuka and Mari) share many similarities, but I think that one that is very possible of being overlooked is the functions of the MAGI supercomputers. Taken from the Evangelion Wiki: "The S.C. Magi System (マギ) are a trio of supercomputers designed by Dr. Naoko Akagi during her research into bio-computers while at Gehirn. The Magi's 7th generation organic computers were implanted with three differing aspects of Dr. Naoko Akagi's personality using the Personality Transplant OS (Operating System), being her persona as a woman (Casper-Magi 3), her persona as a mother (Balthasar-Magi 2), and her persona as a scientist (Melchior-Magi 1). The same system is used to operate the Evangelions. The Magi are used during Episode 13 to connect the simulation bodies to the Evas in their cages and, presumably, the synch-tests when the pilots are not present in the actual Evas." The thing that makes this stick IMO is that Rei, Asuka and Mari all have defining features that set each other apart. Rei is calculated, and as shown in the anime, seems keen on reading, which seems more of what a scientist would do. Asuka is very emotion-driven, whether its her fiery passion to succeed in piloting the Eva or her inability to focus for whatever reason, which women would have a harder time doing in puberty. Mari seems to have a more vast pool of knowledge than the other pilots, as she could be older, which gives her a more motherly feel. But if that's the case for them all, how do they pilot the Evas? Its said that they are used to operate them. I believe that the women are indeed "keys" to unlocking the Evas, along with synchronization, kind of like the missing piece of the MAGI layout. The Eva seems to hold the other pieces of the puzzle. Along with their separate personality, each pilot/Eva shows the following characteristics while fighting: Rei - Scientist Rei(Eva) - Motherly - Protects/Assists the other pilots (Using her hands to hold the core while Asuka stabbed it in 2.0, Throwing Mari out the way while fighting Zeruel in 2.0) Womanly - Protecting the males of the series (Shinji while fighting Ramiel) Asuka - Woman Asuka(Eva) - Scientist - Wants her battles to be as calculated and efficient as possible (Her words after defeating the splitting angel in the TV Series, Ep. 9) Mother - This one actually is a bit tricky, but I think that the hidden feature of Unit 02, the Beast transformation is an example. As many mothers in nature, they become a beast/ferocious when threatened or when needed to protect something, such as what Mari did, no matter the outcome. Mari - Mother Mari (Eva) - Woman - Her personality seems to change in the Eva than outside of it, as she is a lot more cheerful. However, there are random moments (talking to Shinji after their battle in 3.0, as well as Rei during that same time) where she seems to lose her calm demeanor and gets angry and spirited. Mood Swings perhaps? Scientist - Her traits for knowing the intricacies of Unit 02 such as Beast Mode though she has no prior (from what we know) knowledge of it and her skill with a Long Range weapon could show her ability to calculate and dissect information. Something I also want to talk about is how every Impact is caused. While it primarily involves a Lilith and Adam, in a more general and basic/symbolic sense it involves man and woman. Shinji seemed to be a trigger to Impact. Impact maybe could only be caused by a "complete" Adam or a "complete" Lilith. Maybe this whole "each woman has 1 part of the MAGI" theory could also be a fail-safe, to ensure that if the EVA was to ever be taken or compromised for whatever reason, they couldn't be used for Impact without the pilot completing the trifecta? It would hold up under Kwarou as well, as he is an Angel, a being closely equivalent to God or A God, an omnipotent being. That could mean he possess all qualities of humans, which includes the MAGI. [/list]

[]:             Quote Post by artesox » Thu Apr 03, 2014 5:40 pm I think this is more of a coincidence with common topics in EVA instead of a Magi-Nami thing. It is true that Mari and Rei have been helping around the pilots, but so has Misato, and I would like to point that at least on my opinion Mari is the less motherly of the helper trio haha (Meanwhile the other two are stronger and more "active" Mari usually just baits the person into thinking about what is happening, just truly taking an active role when things are beyond desperate) Anyway, my point is, I can't truly see the well defined boundaries between the pilots and the Magi division. Asuka surely is almost fully on Woman, despite her diploma as a scientist. Rei wanders both on Motherly and Scientist, and Mari sincerely walks on the three sides, having shown independence and ambition (related to woman) care (related to mother) and logical thinking and knowledge (related to scientist). Misato also fits well the three sides of magi, and funny enough has three personas/personalities to fit each one (Just change scientist to her cold act as a captain) and for me that kinda breaks the theory of it being a pilot thing only
